# Code of conduct

## The standard

Project Kennel is a working space for building a security tool. Respectful, professional conduct is expected in all project spaces — issues, pull requests, discussions, reviews, and any other channel the project uses.

Treat people the way you would want to be treated in a code review: directly, honestly, and without contempt. Disagree with ideas as sharply as you like; do not attack people. The strictness of the project's contribution rules ([CONTRIBUTING.md](CONTRIBUTING.md)) is about code quality, never about who is contributing.

## Unacceptable behaviour

- Harassment, intimidation, or sustained disruption.
- Personal attacks, demeaning comments, or discrimination on any protected characteristic.
- Doxxing or publishing others' private information.
- Deliberately filing misleading reports, spam, or low-effort contribution at a volume intended to waste maintainer attention.

Maintainers act on these regardless of the technical quality of the contributor's work. A brilliant patch does not buy a pass on conduct.

## Scope

This applies in all project spaces and when an individual is representing the project in public spaces.

## Enforcement

Report conduct concerns to the maintainers (see [MAINTAINERS.md](../docs/governance/MAINTAINERS.md); a dedicated contact is *[TBD]*). Maintainers will review and respond. Responses range from a warning, to temporary restriction, to a permanent ban from project spaces, at maintainer discretion and proportionate to the behaviour. Maintainers who cannot act impartially on a given report recuse themselves.

## Attribution

This code of conduct is intentionally short and project-specific. It is not a fork of a standard template; if the project later adopts a formal external code of conduct, that change will be noted in [CHANGELOG.md](../CHANGELOG.md).
